Rotor Systems.description: Main rotor has two all-metal blades with coning and teeter hinges using self-lubricated bearings. Tail rotor has two all-metal blades with teetering hub and fixed coning angle.
key_points: Main rotor pitch change bearings are oil-filled and sealed with elastomeric boots
Droop stops provide teeter hinge friction restraint during start/stop
Blades have stainless steel spars resistant to corrosion and erosion
Bond line damage possible if exposed at skin-to-spar junction

Drive System.description: Vee-belts transmit power from engine to upper sheave containing overrunning clutch, which drives main and tail rotors through flexible couplings and gearboxes.
key_points: Main gearbox contains single-stage spiral-bevel gear set with splash lubrication
Tail rotor drive shaft has no support bearings but includes damper bearing
Overrunning clutch allows rotor to freewheel during autorotation

Powerplant.description: Lycoming four-cylinder, horizontally-opposed, air-cooled carbureted engine with direct-drive cooling fan and standard accessories.
key_points: Engine includes wet sump oil system
Equipped with two magnetos and shielded ignition
Squirrel-cage fan provides cylinder and oil cooler cooling
Induction air can be selected from cool or heated sources

Flight Controls.description: Dual controls using push-pull tubes and bellcranks, with center-mounted cyclic, conventional collective with correlated throttle, and direct pedal linkage to tail rotor.
key_points: Collective raises throttle when raised, closes when lowered
Throttle correlation and governor less effective above 4000 feet
Cyclic grip angle adjustable for pilot comfort
All bearings are sealed ball bearings or self-lubricated

Removable Flight Controls.description: Left seat pilot controls can be removed for passenger operations, including cyclic grip, collective stick, and tail rotor pedals.
key_points: Quick-release pin secures removable cyclic grip
Collective uses locking pins that must be fully engaged
Pedals remove by depressing pin and twisting counterclockwise
Plastic cap must cover exposed cyclic tube when grip removed

RPM Governor.description: Electronic governor maintains engine RPM by sensing changes and applying corrective throttle inputs through overridable friction clutch.
key_points: Active only above 80% engine RPM
Toggle switch on right collective controls on/off
May not prevent over/under-speed from aggressive maneuvers
Response rate may be too slow at high density altitudes

Control Trim and Friction.description: Balancing trim springs in cyclic and collective controls reduce pilot workload, with adjustable friction devices for both controls.
key_points: Collective-up spring balances rotor loads
Fixed longitudinal bungee cancels cruise stick forces
Lateral trim spring actuated by push-pull knob
Excessive friction can make helicopter difficult to control

Engine Controls.description: Twist-grip throttles on each collective are interconnected and correlated with collective movement, with overtravel spring for autorotation landings.
key_points: Collective raises throttle when raised via correlation linkage
Governor makes minor throttle adjustments automatically
Overtravel spring allows throttle roll-off beyond idle stop
Manual throttle needed for start, shutdown, and emergencies

Clutch Actuator.description: Electric actuator tensions vee-belts by raising upper drive sheave when clutch switch engaged, with automatic tension sensing and caution light indication.
key_points: Blades should begin turning within 5 seconds of engagement
Clutch light on when actuator energized or belts not properly tensioned
Fuse prevents motor overload from tripping circuit breaker
Never take off with clutch caution light on

Fuel System.description: Gravity-flow system with main tank, optional auxiliary tank, shutoff valve, and gascolator, with flexible bladders in aluminum enclosures on later aircraft.
key_points: No fuel pumps - completely gravity fed
Auxiliary tank empties first due to higher position
Low fuel light actuated by separate sender in main tank
Fuel should be sampled from all drains before first flight

Electrical System.description: 14-volt DC system with alternator, sealed lead-acid battery, circuit breakers, and battery relay for system protection.
key_points: Battery located in engine compartment or under console
Tachometers and clock receive power with battery switch off
ALT light indicates low voltage/alternator failure
Do not operate alternator with battery off

Lighting System.description: Anti-collision, position, instrument, map, and dual landing lights provide visibility and collision avoidance.
key_points: Red anti-collision light on tailcone controlled by strobe switch
Landing lights only operate with clutch engaged
Two landing lights at different angles increase coverage
Continuous operation recommended for collision avoidance

Audio System.description: Voice-activated intercom/audio system with VOX squelch control, music input capability, and separate radio/intercom volume controls.
key_points: VOX squelch sets activation threshold
Music input muted during intercom/radio activity
Trigger switches have two detents - intercom then transmit
Earlier systems have PTT/hot mic toggle switch

Pitot-Static System.description: Provides air pressure for airspeed indicator, altimeter, and vertical speed indicator with pitot tube on mast fairing and static source in aft cowling.
key_points: Water can be drained via plugs under cabin
Sources should be inspected for bugs/obstructions
Draining only required if instruments appear erratic

Dual Tachometer.description: Electronic engine and rotor tachometer with separate circuits, receiving signals from magneto points and magnetic senders.
key_points: Each tachometer has own circuit breaker
Tachometers powered with battery/alternator off if clutch engaged
Do not stow with clutch engaged to prevent battery discharge

Warning and Caution Lights.description: Multiple warning/caution lights monitor critical systems including clutch, gearboxes, engine, fuel, and environmental conditions.
key_points: Low RPM light/horn activate at 97% rotor RPM or below
Chip lights detect metallic particles indicating potential failure
Main gearbox temp light warns of overheating
Full throttle light indicates engine near maximum power

Audio Alerts.description: Low-RPM horn and high-RPM warble tone provide audio warnings through speaker or headsets.
key_points: Low-RPM horn sounds below 97% rotor RPM
Horn muted when collective fully down
High-RPM warble tone warns approaching 110% rotor RPM
Test button allows pre-flight testing of alerts

Engine Monitoring Unit.description: Digital recording device continuously monitors engine/rotor parameters and records exceedances for maintenance purposes.
key_points: Monitors RPM, temperatures, pressures continuously
Amber light indicates status - steady is normal
Fast flashing indicates detected exceedance
Data downloadable but pilot must still report exceedances

Cabin Heating and Ventilation.description: Fresh air vents in doors and nose provide ventilation, with optional heater using muffler heat shroud.
key_points: Door vents should be wide open in hover, 1 inch in cruise
Nose inlet air directed along windshield for defogging
Heater control must be pushed off in engine fire
Later aircraft use engine cooling air instead of electric blower

Seats, Belts, and Baggage.description: Non-adjustable seats with removable back cushion, combined lap belt/shoulder harness, and under-seat baggage compartments.
key_points: Back cushion positions shorter pilots forward
Inertia reel locks during sudden movement
Baggage compartments accessed by hinging seat cushions forward

Landing Gear.description: Skid-type landing gear with energy-absorbing crosstube that yields in hard landings to protect occupants.
key_points: Struts hinge up and outward as crosstube yields
Tail skid clearance indicates crosstube condition
Wear shoes must be replaced when less than 0.05 inches thick

Rotor Brake.description: Cable-actuated brake mounted on main gearbox stops rotor after shutdown and serves as parking brake.
key_points: Wait 30 seconds after mixture off before applying
Use moderate force (10 lb) to avoid damaging shoes
Starter disabled when brake engaged
Can be used as parking brake with bead chain

Engine Primer System.description: Manual primer pump improves cold engine starting by injecting fuel directly into intake system.
key_points: Located near hourmeter in front of right seat
Normally requires 2-3 strokes for priming
Pull handle slowly to allow fuel to fill pump
Must be locked after priming

Carbon Monoxide Detector.description: Sensor above pilot's heater outlet detects elevated CO levels with caution light warning.
key_points: System check performed at power-on
Continuing flash indicates sensor malfunction
If light illuminates, shut off heater and ventilate
Many chemicals can damage sensor

ADS-B Equipment.description: Transponder transmits GPS position to ATC, with optional receive capability for traffic and weather information.
key_points: ADS-B Out required in certain airspace
Factory equipment operates on 1090 MHz
ADS-B In requires suitable display like GPS or MFD
Should not be turned off unless directed by ATC

Emergency Locator Transmitter.description: Automatic activation transmitter with remote cockpit switch transmits emergency signal when subjected to high G-load.
key_points: Transmitter switch secured in AUTO/ARM position
Remote switch should be AUTO for flight
ON position for imminent emergency landing
RESET position stops inadvertent activation

Accessory Mounts.description: Mounting bars with clamp assemblies provide attachment points for portable devices with power outlets.
key_points: 10 lb weight limit per bar
USB and cigarette-lighter power outlets provided
Items must not interfere with controls or view
Pilot responsible for any attached items

Cockpit Camera.description: Ceiling-mounted 4K video camera records video, audio, and GPS position to internal memory and removable flash drive.
key_points: Recording starts automatically with battery on
Blue flashing light indicates recording to flash drive
128 GB drive records approximately 10 hours
Do not remove drive while recording
